Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 5, 308 [loan of dates][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[...] ZU₂.LUM.MA ⸢ZA₃.LU⸣ [...]

(o 1) [...] dates for the lease [...] son of Nanaya-iddin [...] and Anu-ah-ittannu ... beside the house [...] PN, son of PN, incumbent on Rihat-Anu, son of [PN] and Šamaš-zer-lišir son of Anu-... [...]. On the month of Tašritu, the 13th day, Seleucus the king.

o 22

[...] ⸢DUMU?⸣ m⸢d⸣na-na-a—⸢MU⸣ [...]

o 33

[... m?d?60?]—⸢ŠEŠ?⸣—it-tan-nu xu₂ U₂ TA ⸢E₂?⸣ x

o 44

⸢m⸣x-x-x-x DUMU ša₂ mx-x—MU ina muḫ-ḫi

o 55

ša₂ <m>ri-ḫat—d60 DUMU ša₂ [...]-x u mdUTU—NUMUN—⸢GIŠ⸣

o 66

DUMU ša₂ md60—x-[...] x [...] x [...]

o 77

ina itiDU₆ MU 13!-KAM₂ msi-lu-ku LUGAL

o 88

⸢ZU₂⸣.LUM.MA a₄ 1(U) 4(AŠ) GUR <ina> 1et ⸢rit⸣-tu₂ ina ma-ši-ḫu

(o 8) These dates of 14 kurru in one installment in the measure (containing) 1 pānu, 3 qû (for a) price of x shekels for 1 kurru [...]. The second part of the owners is a load of [...].

o 99

ša₂ 1 PI 3 SILA₃ ši-im <x> GIN₂ <KU₃.BABBAR> KI 1(AŠ) ⸢GUR⸣ x [...]

o 1010

⸢ḪA⸣.LA EN-MEŠ ša₂-ni-tu₄ GUNtu₄ ša₂ EŠ?-x-[...]

Reverse
r 1r 1

tu-ḫal-lu₄ lib₂!-bi₂ u man-ga-⸢ga⸣ [...]

(r 1) They will give palm-leaf basket, palm heart1 and fibre of the date palm [...] in the dates delivered place. They are interdependent for the payment. Iqiša will be paid where he wants. Apart from a special date palm of Adad, the fallow lands of Nanaya, those gardens (and) arable lands are put at their disposal.

r 22

ina ḫa-ṣa-ri ina-an-din-ʾ pu-ut a-ḫa-miš

r 33

a-na SUR na-šu-u₂ a-šar ša₂ mBAša₂-a ṣe-bu-u₂ in-niṭSUR

r 44

e-lat 1(DIŠ)en gišGIŠIMMAR ia-a-ḫu-du ša₂ dIŠKUR ša₂ ina IGI!-šu₂-nu

r 55

⸢KI.KAL⸣-MEŠuʾ ša₂ dna-na-a KIRI₆ ŠE.NUMUN MU-MEŠ ip-pu-uš-ʾ

r 66

lu₂mu-kin₇ mta-nit-tu₄—d60 DUMU ša₂ mtat-tan-nu

(r 6) Witnesses : Tanittu-Anu, son of Tattannu, Anu-zer-iddin, son of Nanaya-iddin, Labaši, son of Nanaya-iddin, Anu-ab-uṣur, son of Ina-qibit-Anu, Ištar-hiṭua, son of Mannu-ki-Delebat. Nidintu-Anu, the scribe, son of Nanaya-iddin. At Uruk, month Ululu, day 10, year 11, Seleucus the king.

r 77

md60—NUMUN—MU DUMU ša₂ mdna-na-a—MU

r 88

mla-ba-ši DUMU ša₂ mdna-na-a—MU

r 99

md60—AD—URI₃ DUMU ša₂ mina—⸢qi₂⸣-bit—d60

r 1010

mdINANA—ḫi-ṭu-u₂-a DUMU ša₂ mman-nu—ki₂-i—ddele-bat

r 1111

mni-din-tu₄—d60 lu₂UMBISAG DUMU ša₂ mdna-na-a—MU

Top
t.e. 1t.e. 1

UNUGki itiKIN U₄ 10-KAM₂ MU 11-KAM₂ msi-lu-ku LUGAL

t.e. 22

ṣu-pur mri-ḫat—d60

(t.e. 2) Nail of Rihat-Anu and Anu-zer-lišir.

2 nail impressions
t.e. 33

u md60—⸢NUMUN—GIŠ⸣

Bottom
b.e. 1b.e. 1

un-⸢qa⸣ un-qa un-qa

(b.e. 1) Seal: Anu-zer-iddin. Seal: Anu-ab-uṣur. Seal: Anu-balassu-iqbi.

traces of 3 seal impressions
b.e. 22

md60—NUMUN—MU md60—AD—URI₃ md60—DIN-su!—[E]

Left
l.e. 1l.e. 1

un-⸢qa⸣ un-qa

(l.e. 1) Seal: Tannittu-Anu. Seal: PN.

traces of 1 seal impression and 1 winged horse
l.e. 22

[m]ta-nit-tu₄—d60 m[...]-x-x-[...]

1 These two graphemes can also be read lip2-pi for 'wrapping ' but parallels show lib3-bi (CAD T , p.452a).
